packages.name: qa-gate
packages.description: Independent QA audits: tasks reaching review get a one-shot auditor that re-verifies the criteria; failures bounce back with findings. Ships its own QA-officer agent you can chat with.

packages.name: session-bell
packages.description: Notifications when any session finishes or asks for input; adds a ping_all_sessions tool for Master.

packages.name: cost-pulse
packages.description: Live per-session spend bar chart as a tab.

packages.name: kanban-lite
packages.description: The built-in kanban board as a pure addon (proof of the addon platform).

packages.name: workflows
packages.description: Task workflows as state machines — steps with on-done / on-fail transitions, remediation branches, visit-capped retry loops. Workflow list with pass rates and pause toggles, drag-and-drop canvas editor with labeled transitions, cron triggers, and replayable run history.

packages.name: github-issues
packages.description: A GitHub issue triage inbox — sync open issues from any number of repos on a schedule, spawn linked board tasks (or let the customizable triage agent decide), track their progress, and optionally close issues on GitHub when their task is done. Token stays in the OS keychain.

packages.name: usage-limit-rescheduler
packages.description: Tasks that failed on an API usage/rate limit get automatically rescheduled for right after the limit resets — detected from session output or the Anthropic usage API.

packages.name: dev-kitchen-sink
packages.description: Developer reference — exercises every addon capability (state, sessions, tasks with review verbs, templates, schedules, storage, allowlisted HTTP, keychain secrets, the addon agent, UI calls, all hooks, a Master tool). Built on the toolkit.

agents.name: Release Captain
agents.description: Owns the path from merged work to a shippable release: living release notes, a gate checklist mini app, weekly readiness sweeps, and a dashboard showing blockers at a glance.

agents.name: News Briefer
agents.description: Researches your topics every weekday morning, cross-checks sources, files the digest under knowledge/, and keeps the headlines on its dashboard.
